---
title: "CFMG Economic Transformation"
author: "Rhett D Harrison"
format:
        html:
                css: custom.css
        pdf: default
filters:
- collapse-output
editor: visual
---

```{r}
#| label: Load_data_and_libraries
#| include: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false

library(tidyverse)
library(grid)
library(gt)
library(ggplot2)
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(emmeans)
library(scales)
library(broom.mixed)
library(modelsummary)
library(ordinal)
library(tidytext)
library(ggwordcloud)
library(ggbreak)
library(patchwork)

load("cfmg.RData")
```

## Introduction
Here we consider the effect of CFMG management on indicators of economic transformation.

### Access to natural resources
We asked FGD groups whether CFM had improved access to natural resources for household use or for sale (@fig-et_household_resources, @fig-et_resources_for_sale).

```{r}
#| label: fig-et_household_resources
#| code-fold: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| fig-cap: "Responses to whether improved access to natural resources for household use is an economic benefit of CFM. The length of each bar segment represents the proportion of groups in that category. The numbers superimposed on the bars represent the actual number of groups in each category."

cfmg |>
  filter(interview_type != "individual_interview") |>
  mutate(
    response = case_match(
      as.character(economic_transformation_economic_benefits_from_cfm_improved_access_to_natural_resources_for_household_use_e_g_firewood_building_materials),
      "1" ~ "Yes",
      "0" ~ "No"
    ),
    response = factor(response, levels = c("Yes", "No")),
    interview_type = factor(
      case_match(
        interview_type,
        "cfmg_executive" ~ "Executive",
        "cfmg_female"    ~ "Female",
        "cfmg_male"      ~ "Male",
        "cfmg_youth"     ~ "Youth"
      ),
      levels = c("Youth", "Male", "Female", "Executive")
    )
  ) |>
  drop_na(response) |>
  count(interview_type, response) |>
  group_by(interview_type) |>
  mutate(prop = n / sum(n)) |>
  ungroup() |>
  ggplot(aes(y = interview_type, x = prop, fill = response)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.65, colour = "white", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = ifelse(prop >= 0.05, n, "")),
    position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5),
    size = 3.5, fontface = "bold", colour = "white"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1),
    expand = expansion(mult = 0.01),
    name   = "Proportion of groups"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("Yes" = "#2A9D8F", "No" = "#E07B54"),
    guide  = guide_legend(reverse = TRUE)
  ) +
  labs(y = NULL, fill = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    axis.line.x.bottom = element_line(colour = "grey30", linewidth = 0.5),
    axis.text.y        = element_text(size = rel(1.14), colour = "grey20"),
    axis.text.x        = element_text(size = rel(1.14), colour = "grey20"),
    axis.title.x       = element_text(size = rel(1), colour = "grey40"),
    legend.position    = "bottom",
    legend.text        = element_text(size = rel(1)),
    plot.margin        = margin(10, 25, 10, 10)
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-et_resources_for_sale
#| code-fold: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| fig-cap: "Responses to whether improved access to natural resources for sale is an economic benefit of CFM. The length of each bar segment represents the proportion of groups in that category. The numbers superimposed on the bars represent the actual number of groups in each category."

cfmg |>
  filter(interview_type != "individual_interview") |>
  mutate(
    response = case_match(
      as.character(economic_transformation_economic_benefits_from_cfm_improved_access_to_natural_resources_for_sale_e_g_timber_honey_mushrooms),
      "1" ~ "Yes",
      "0" ~ "No"
    ),
    response = factor(response, levels = c("Yes", "No")),
    interview_type = factor(
      case_match(
        interview_type,
        "cfmg_executive" ~ "Executive",
        "cfmg_female"    ~ "Female",
        "cfmg_male"      ~ "Male",
        "cfmg_youth"     ~ "Youth"
      ),
      levels = c("Youth", "Male", "Female", "Executive")
    )
  ) |>
  drop_na(response) |>
  count(interview_type, response) |>
  group_by(interview_type) |>
  mutate(prop = n / sum(n)) |>
  ungroup() |>
  ggplot(aes(y = interview_type, x = prop, fill = response)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.65, colour = "white", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = ifelse(prop >= 0.05, n, "")),
    position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5),
    size = 3.5, fontface = "bold", colour = "white"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1),
    expand = expansion(mult = 0.01),
    name   = "Proportion of groups"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("Yes" = "#2A9D8F", "No" = "#E07B54"),
    guide  = guide_legend(reverse = TRUE)
  ) +
  labs(y = NULL, fill = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    axis.line.x.bottom = element_line(colour = "grey30", linewidth = 0.5),
    axis.text.y        = element_text(size = rel(1.14), colour = "grey20"),
    axis.text.x        = element_text(size = rel(1.14), colour = "grey20"),
    axis.title.x       = element_text(size = rel(1), colour = "grey40"),
    legend.position    = "bottom",
    legend.text        = element_text(size = rel(1)),
    plot.margin        = margin(10, 25, 10, 10)
  )
```

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
### Access to natural resources - FGDs - Model methods
To examine differences among FGD types in whether they reported improved access to natural resources as an economic benefit of CFM, we employed a generalised linear mixed model (GLMM) with a binomial response and random intercepts for province and CFMG nested within province. We used FGD type as the factor. We ran separate models for household use and resources for sale. We examined the significance of pairwise differences among FGD types using Tukey HSD.
:::

```{r}
#| label: model_et_fgd_household
#| code-fold: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false

test.data <- cfmg |>
  filter(interview_type != "individual_interview") |>
  mutate(
    hh = economic_transformation_economic_benefits_from_cfm_improved_access_to_natural_resources_for_household_use_e_g_firewood_building_materials,
    interview_type = factor(
      case_match(
        interview_type,
        "cfmg_executive" ~ "Executive",
        "cfmg_female"    ~ "Female",
        "cfmg_male"      ~ "Male",
        "cfmg_youth"     ~ "Youth"
      ),
      levels = c("Executive", "Female", "Male", "Youth")
    )
  ) |>
  drop_na(hh) |>
  mutate(province_cfmg = interaction(province, cfmg_name))

M1 <- glmer(hh ~ interview_type + (1 | province) + (1 | province_cfmg),
            data = test.data, family = binomial)

emm.M1      <- emmeans(M1, specs = ~ interview_type, type = "response")
pairwise.M1 <- pairs(emm.M1, adjust = "Tukey")
```

```{r}
#| label: M1_et_fgd_household
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| output-fold: true
#| output-summary: "Household resources – FGD model results (click to expand)"

summary(M1)
```

```{r}
#| label: pairwise_et_fgd_household
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| output-fold: true
#| output-summary: "Household resources – FGD pairwise comparisons (click to expand)"

summary(pairwise.M1)
```

```{r}
#| label: model_et_fgd_sale
#| code-fold: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false

test.data <- cfmg |>
  filter(interview_type != "individual_interview") |>
  mutate(
    sale = economic_transformation_economic_benefits_from_cfm_improved_access_to_natural_resources_for_sale_e_g_timber_honey_mushrooms,
    interview_type = factor(
      case_match(
        interview_type,
        "cfmg_executive" ~ "Executive",
        "cfmg_female"    ~ "Female",
        "cfmg_male"      ~ "Male",
        "cfmg_youth"     ~ "Youth"
      ),
      levels = c("Executive", "Female", "Male", "Youth")
    )
  ) |>
  drop_na(sale) |>
  mutate(province_cfmg = interaction(province, cfmg_name))

M2 <- glmer(sale ~ interview_type + (1 | province) + (1 | province_cfmg),
            data = test.data, family = binomial)

emm.M2      <- emmeans(M2, specs = ~ interview_type, type = "response")
pairwise.M2 <- pairs(emm.M2, adjust = "Tukey")
```

```{r}
#| label: M2_et_fgd_sale
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| output-fold: true
#| output-summary: "Resources for sale – FGD model results (click to expand)"

summary(M2)
```

```{r}
#| label: pairwise_et_fgd_sale
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| output-fold: true
#| output-summary: "Resources for sale – FGD pairwise comparisons (click to expand)"

summary(pairwise.M2)
```

Individual interviewees were also asked whether CFM had improved access to natural resources for household use or for sale. @tbl-et_individual_access shows the proportion reporting each, broken down by interviewee role.

```{r}
#| label: tbl-et_individual_access
#| code-fold: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| tbl-cap: "Percentage of individual interviewees reporting improved access to natural resources as an economic benefit of CFM by interviewee role."

cfmg |>
  filter(interview_type == "individual_interview") |>
  select(
    interviewee_role,
    hh   = economic_transformation_economic_benefits_from_cfm_improved_access_to_natural_resources_for_household_use_e_g_firewood_building_materials,
    sale = economic_transformation_economic_benefits_from_cfm_improved_access_to_natural_resources_for_sale_e_g_timber_honey_mushrooms
  ) |>
  mutate(
    interviewee_role = case_match(
      as.character(interviewee_role),
      "cfmg_member"      ~ "Community member",
      "cfmg_exec_member" ~ "CFMG executive member",
      "trad_leader"      ~ "Traditional leader",
      "dfo"              ~ "District Forestry Officer",
      "NGO_rep"          ~ "NGO representative"
    ),
    interviewee_role = factor(interviewee_role, levels = c(
      "Community member", "CFMG executive member", "Traditional leader",
      "District Forestry Officer", "NGO representative"
    ))
  ) |>
  group_by(interviewee_role) |>
  summarise(
    n         = n(),
    Household = mean(hh,   na.rm = TRUE),
    Sale      = mean(sale, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups   = "drop"
  ) |>
  arrange(interviewee_role) |>
  gt() |>
  cols_label(
    interviewee_role = "Role",
    n                = "n",
    Household        = "Household use",
    Sale             = "For sale"
  ) |>
  fmt_percent(
    columns  = c(Household, Sale),
    decimals = 0
  ) |>
  tab_header(
    title    = "Improved access to natural resources from CFM as reported by individual interviewees",
    subtitle = "Values represent the % of responses agreeing that CFM has improved assess to resources for household use or sale. n = the total number of interview responses. A positive response indicates that CFM has improved access to natural resources for household use or for sale."
  ) |>
  opt_align_table_header(align = "left") |>
  cols_align(align = "left",   columns = interviewee_role) |>
  cols_align(align = "center", columns = c(n, Household, Sale)) |>
  tab_style(
    style     = cell_text(align = "center"),
    locations = cells_column_labels(columns = interviewee_role)
  ) |>
  sub_missing()
```

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
### Access to natural resources - Individual interviewees - Model methods
To examine differences among individual interviewee roles in whether they reported improved access to natural resources as an economic benefit of CFM, we employed a generalised linear mixed model (GLMM) with a binomial response and random intercepts for province and CFMG nested within province. We included interviewee role, interviewee gender and interviewee age as factors. We ran separate models for household use and resources for sale. To examine pairwise differences among interviewee roles, we employed Tukey HSD.
:::

```{r}
#| label: model_et_individual_household
#| code-fold: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false

test.data <- cfmg |>
  filter(interview_type == "individual_interview") |>
  mutate(
    hh                 = economic_transformation_economic_benefits_from_cfm_improved_access_to_natural_resources_for_household_use_e_g_firewood_building_materials,
    interviewee_gender = demo_information_interviewee_gender,
    interviewee_age    = demo_information_interviewee_age
  ) |>
  drop_na(hh) |>
  mutate(province_cfmg = interaction(province, cfmg_name))

M4 <- glmer(hh ~ interviewee_role + interviewee_gender + interviewee_age +
              (1 | province) + (1 | province_cfmg),
            data = test.data, family = binomial)

emm.M4      <- emmeans(M4, specs = ~ interviewee_role, type = "response")
pairwise.M4 <- pairs(emm.M4, adjust = "Tukey")
```

```{r}
#| label: M4_et_individual_household
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| output-fold: true
#| output-summary: "Household resources – Individual model results (click to expand)"

summary(M4)
```

```{r}
#| label: pairwise_et_individual_household
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| output-fold: true
#| output-summary: "Household resources – Individual pairwise comparisons (click to expand)"

summary(pairwise.M4)
```

```{r}
#| label: model_et_individual_sale
#| code-fold: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false

test.data <- cfmg |>
  filter(interview_type == "individual_interview") |>
  mutate(
    sale               = economic_transformation_economic_benefits_from_cfm_improved_access_to_natural_resources_for_sale_e_g_timber_honey_mushrooms,
    interviewee_gender = demo_information_interviewee_gender,
    interviewee_age    = demo_information_interviewee_age
  ) |>
  drop_na(sale) |>
  mutate(province_cfmg = interaction(province, cfmg_name))

M5 <- glmer(sale ~ interviewee_role + interviewee_gender + interviewee_age +
              (1 | province) + (1 | province_cfmg),
            data = test.data, family = binomial)

emm.M5      <- emmeans(M5, specs = ~ interviewee_role, type = "response")
pairwise.M5 <- pairs(emm.M5, adjust = "Tukey")
```

```{r}
#| label: M5_et_individual_sale
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| output-fold: true
#| output-summary: "Resources for sale – Individual model results (click to expand)"

summary(M5)
```

```{r}
#| label: pairwise_et_individual_sale
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| output-fold: true
#| output-summary: "Resources for sale – Individual pairwise comparisons (click to expand)"

summary(pairwise.M5)
```

### Employment
We asked FGD groups whether CFM had generated new employment opportunities (@fig-et_employment).

```{r}
#| label: fig-et_employment
#| code-fold: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| fig-cap: "Responses to whether CFM has generated employment opportunities. The length of each bar segment represents the proportion of groups in that category. The numbers superimposed on the bars represent the actual number of groups in each category."

cfmg |>
  filter(interview_type != "individual_interview") |>
  mutate(
    response = economic_transformation_yn_formal_employment_opportunities_created_cfm,
    response = case_match(response,
                          "yes" ~ "Yes",
                          "no" ~ "No",
                          .default = NA),
    response = factor(response, levels = c("Yes", "No")),
    interview_type = factor(
      case_match(
        interview_type,
        "cfmg_executive" ~ "Executive",
        "cfmg_female"    ~ "Female",
        "cfmg_male"      ~ "Male",
        "cfmg_youth"     ~ "Youth"
      ),
      levels = c("Youth", "Male", "Female", "Executive")
    )
  ) |>
  drop_na(response) |>
  count(interview_type, response) |>
  group_by(interview_type) |>
  mutate(prop = n / sum(n)) |>
  ungroup() |>
  ggplot(aes(y = interview_type, x = prop, fill = response)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.65, colour = "white", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = ifelse(prop >= 0.05, n, "")),
    position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5),
    size = 3.5, fontface = "bold", colour = "white"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1),
    expand = expansion(mult = 0.01),
    name   = "Proportion of groups"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("Yes" = "#2A9D8F", "No" = "#E07B54"),
    guide  = guide_legend(reverse = TRUE)
  ) +
  labs(y = NULL, fill = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    axis.line.x.bottom = element_line(colour = "grey30", linewidth = 0.5),
    axis.text.y        = element_text(size = rel(1.14), colour = "grey20"),
    axis.text.x        = element_text(size = rel(1.14), colour = "grey20"),
    axis.title.x       = element_text(size = rel(1), colour = "grey40"),
    legend.position    = "bottom",
    legend.text        = element_text(size = rel(1)),
    plot.margin        = margin(10, 25, 10, 10)
  )
```

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
### Employment - FGDs - Model methods
To examine differences among FGD types in whether they reported CFM had generated employment opportunities, we employed a generalised linear mixed model (GLMM) with a binomial response and random intercepts for province and CFMG nested within province. We used FGD type as the factor. We examined the significance of pairwise differences among FGD types using Tukey HSD.
:::

```{r}
#| label: model_et_fgd_employment
#| code-fold: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false

test.data <- cfmg |>
  filter(interview_type != "individual_interview") |>
  mutate(
    empl = economic_transformation_yn_formal_employment_opportunities_created_cfm,
    empl = case_match(empl,
                      "yes" ~ 1,
                      "no" ~ 0,
                      .default = NA),
    interview_type = factor(
      case_match(
        interview_type,
        "cfmg_executive" ~ "Executive",
        "cfmg_female"    ~ "Female",
        "cfmg_male"      ~ "Male",
        "cfmg_youth"     ~ "Youth"
      ),
      levels = c("Executive", "Female", "Male", "Youth")
    )
  ) |>
  drop_na(empl) |>
  mutate(province_cfmg = interaction(province, cfmg_name))

M3 <- glmer(empl ~ interview_type + (1 | province) + (1 | province_cfmg),
            data = test.data, family = binomial)

emm.M3      <- emmeans(M3, specs = ~ interview_type, type = "response")
pairwise.M3 <- pairs(emm.M3, adjust = "Tukey")
```

```{r}
#| label: M3_et_fgd_employment
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| output-fold: true
#| output-summary: "Employment – FGD model results (click to expand)"

summary(M3)
```

```{r}
#| label: pairwise_et_fgd_employment
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| output-fold: true
#| output-summary: "Employment – FGD pairwise comparisons (click to expand)"

summary(pairwise.M3)
```

Individual interviewees were also asked whether CFM had generated employment opportunities from difference sources. @tbl-et_individual_employment shows the proportion reporting this, broken down by interviewee role.

```{r}
#| label: tbl-et_individual_employment
#| code-fold: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| tbl-cap: "Percentage of individual interviewees reporting that CFM has generated employment opportunities by interviewee role."

cfmg |>
  filter(interview_type == "individual_interview") |>
  select(
    interviewee_role,
    empl = economic_transformation_yn_formal_employment_opportunities_created_cfm
  ) |>
  mutate(
    interviewee_role = case_match(
      as.character(interviewee_role),
      "cfmg_member"      ~ "Community member",
      "cfmg_exec_member" ~ "CFMG executive member",
      "trad_leader"      ~ "Traditional leader",
      "dfo"              ~ "District Forestry Officer",
      "NGO_rep"          ~ "NGO representative"
    ),
    interviewee_role = factor(interviewee_role, levels = c(
      "Community member", "CFMG executive member", "Traditional leader",
      "District Forestry Officer", "NGO representative"
    )),
    empl = case_match(empl,
                      "yes" ~ 1,
                      "no" ~ 0,
                      .default = NA)
    
  ) |>
  group_by(interviewee_role) |>
  summarise(
    n          = n(),
    Employment = mean(empl, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups    = "drop"
  ) |>
  arrange(interviewee_role) |>
  gt() |>
  cols_label(
    interviewee_role = "Role",
    n                = "n",
    Employment       = "Agree CFM has generated employment opportunities"
  ) |>
  fmt_percent(
    columns  = Employment,
    decimals = 0
  ) |>
  tab_header(
    title    = "Employment opportunities reported by individual interviewees",
    subtitle = "Values represent the % of responses agreeing that CFM has generated employment opportunities. n = the total number of interview responses. A positive response indicates that CFM has resulted in new employment opportunities."
  ) |>
  opt_align_table_header(align = "left") |>
  cols_align(align = "left",   columns = interviewee_role) |>
  cols_align(align = "center", columns = c(n, Employment)) |>
  tab_style(
    style     = cell_text(align = "center"),
    locations = cells_column_labels(columns = interviewee_role)
  ) |>
  sub_missing()
```

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
### Employment - Individual interviewees - Model methods
To examine differences among individual interviewee roles in whether they reported CFM had generated employment opportunities, we employed a generalised linear mixed model (GLMM) with a binomial response and random intercepts for province and CFMG nested within province. We included interviewee role, interviewee gender and interviewee age as factors. To examine pairwise differences among interviewee roles, we employed Tukey HSD.
:::

```{r}
#| label: model_et_individual_employment
#| code-fold: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false

test.data <- cfmg |>
  filter(interview_type == "individual_interview") |>
  mutate(
    empl               = economic_transformation_yn_formal_employment_opportunities_created_cfm,
    empl = case_match(empl,
                      "yes" ~ 1,
                      "no" ~ 0,
                      .default = NA),
    interviewee_gender = demo_information_interviewee_gender,
    interviewee_age    = demo_information_interviewee_age
  ) |>
  drop_na(empl) |>
  mutate(province_cfmg = interaction(province, cfmg_name))

M6 <- glmer(empl ~ interviewee_role + interviewee_gender + interviewee_age +
              (1 | province) + (1 | province_cfmg),
            data = test.data, family = binomial)

emm.M6      <- emmeans(M6, specs = ~ interviewee_role, type = "response")
pairwise.M6 <- pairs(emm.M6, adjust = "Tukey")
```

```{r}
#| label: M6_et_individual_employment
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| output-fold: true
#| output-summary: "Employment – Individual model results (click to expand)"

summary(M6)
```

```{r}
#| label: pairwise_et_individual_employment
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| output-fold: true
#| output-summary: "Employment – Individual pairwise comparisons (click to expand)"

summary(pairwise.M6)
```


#### Change in numbers employed
We asked executive FGDs to estimate the number of people employed before and after CFM (@fig-et_num_employed).

```{r}
#| label: fig-et_num_employed
#| code-fold: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| fig-cap: "Mean number of people per CFMG employed before and after CFM by province, as reported by CFMG Executives."
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 5

cfmg |>
  filter(interview_type == "cfmg_executive") |>
  select(
    province,
    "Before CFM" = economic_transformation_num_employed_before_cfmg,
    "After CFM"  = economic_transformation_num_employed_after_cfmg
  ) |>
  pivot_longer(-province, names_to = "timing", values_to = "n_employed") |>
  filter(!is.na(n_employed)) |>
  mutate(
    timing     = factor(timing, levels = c("Before CFM", "After CFM")),
    n_employed = if_else(province == "Western" & timing == "After CFM",
                         n_employed / 2, n_employed)
  ) |>
  group_by(province, timing) |>
  summarise(mean_employed = mean(n_employed, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
  ggplot(aes(y = reorder(province, mean_employed), x = mean_employed, fill = timing)) +
  geom_col(
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.7), width = 0.6,
    colour = "white", linewidth = 0.3
  ) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = round(mean_employed, 0)),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.7),
    hjust = -0.2, size = 3.5, colour = "grey25", fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.18)),
    name   = "Mean number employed per CFMG"
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("Before CFM" = "#E07B54", "After CFM" = "#2A9D8F")
  ) +
  labs(y = NULL, fill = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 18) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_line(colour = "grey85", linewidth = 0.4),
    axis.line.x.bottom = element_line(colour = "grey30", linewidth = 0.5),
    axis.text.y        = element_text(size = rel(1.14), colour = "grey20"),
    axis.text.x        = element_text(size = rel(1.14), colour = "grey20"),
    axis.title.x       = element_text(size = rel(1), colour = "grey40"),
    legend.position    = "bottom",
    legend.text        = element_text(size = rel(1)),
    plot.margin        = margin(10, 25, 10, 10)
  )
```

#### Full-time employment by gender and province
Executive FGDs also reported the number of males and females in full-time employment in the CFMG (@fig-et_fulltime_gender).

```{r}
#| label: fig-et_fulltime_gender
#| code-fold: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| fig-cap: "Number of males and females employed full-time in CFMGs, by province, as reported by executive FGDs."
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 5

cfmg |>
  filter(interview_type == "cfmg_executive") |>
  select(
    province,
    "Male"   = economic_transformation_formal_employment_quests_formalemploymentdetails_1__males_employed_cfmg_full_time,
    "Female" = economic_transformation_formal_employment_quests_formalemploymentdetails_1__females_employed_cfmg_full_time
  ) |>
  pivot_longer(-province, names_to = "gender", values_to = "n_employed") |>
  filter(!is.na(n_employed)) |>
  mutate(gender = factor(gender, levels = c("Male", "Female"))) |>
  group_by(province, gender) |>
  summarise(total = sum(n_employed, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
  ggplot(aes(y = reorder(province, total), x = total, fill = gender)) +
  geom_col(
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.7), width = 0.6,
    colour = "white", linewidth = 0.3
  ) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = total),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.7),
    hjust = -0.2, size = 3.5, colour = "grey25", fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.18)),
    name   = "Number employed full-time"
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("Male" = "#2A9D8F", "Female" = "#E07B54")
  ) +
  labs(y = NULL, fill = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 18) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_line(colour = "grey85", linewidth = 0.4),
    axis.line.x.bottom = element_line(colour = "grey30", linewidth = 0.5),
    axis.text.y        = element_text(size = rel(1.14), colour = "grey20"),
    axis.text.x        = element_text(size = rel(1.14), colour = "grey20"),
    axis.title.x       = element_text(size = rel(1), colour = "grey40"),
    legend.position    = "bottom",
    legend.text        = element_text(size = rel(1)),
    plot.margin        = margin(10, 25, 10, 10)
  )
```

Executive FGDs also reported the number of male and female youth in full-time employment in the CFMG (@fig-et_fulltime_youth).

```{r}
#| label: fig-et_fulltime_youth
#| code-fold: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| fig-cap: "Number of male and female youth employed full-time in CFMGs, by province, as reported by executive FGDs."
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 5

cfmg |>
  filter(interview_type == "cfmg_executive") |>
  select(
    province,
    "Male youth"   = economic_transformation_formal_employment_quests_formalemploymentdetails_1__male_youth_employed_cfmg_full_time,
    "Female youth" = economic_transformation_formal_employment_quests_formalemploymentdetails_1__female_youth_employed_cfmg_full_time
  ) |>
  pivot_longer(-province, names_to = "gender", values_to = "n_employed") |>
  filter(!is.na(n_employed)) |>
  mutate(gender = factor(gender, levels = c("Male youth", "Female youth"))) |>
  group_by(province, gender) |>
  summarise(total = sum(n_employed, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
  ggplot(aes(y = reorder(province, total), x = total, fill = gender)) +
  geom_col(
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.7), width = 0.6,
    colour = "white", linewidth = 0.3
  ) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = total),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.7),
    hjust = -0.2, size = 3.5, colour = "grey25", fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.18)),
    name   = "Number of youth employed full-time"
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("Male youth" = "#2A9D8F", "Female youth" = "#E07B54")
  ) +
  labs(y = NULL, fill = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 18) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_line(colour = "grey85", linewidth = 0.4),
    axis.line.x.bottom = element_line(colour = "grey30", linewidth = 0.5),
    axis.text.y        = element_text(size = rel(1.14), colour = "grey20"),
    axis.text.x        = element_text(size = rel(1.14), colour = "grey20"),
    axis.title.x       = element_text(size = rel(1), colour = "grey40"),
    legend.position    = "bottom",
    legend.text        = element_text(size = rel(1)),
    plot.margin        = margin(10, 25, 10, 10)
  )
```


#### Types of formal employment opportunities
Female and male FGDs identified the types of formal employment opportunities generated by CFM (@fig-et_employment_types).

```{r}
#| label: fig-et_employment_types
#| code-fold: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| fig-cap: "Types of formal employment opportunities reported by female and male FGDs. Word size is proportional to the number of FGDs reporting that job type."
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 7

prefix <- "economic_transformation_formal_employment_opportunities_from_cfm_"

job_labels <- c(
  "forest_managers"                                          = "Forest managers",
  "forest_rangers"                                           = "Forest rangers",
  "community_scouts"                                         = "Community scouts",
  "hfos"                                                     = "HFOs",
  "monitoring_and_evaluation_officers"                       = "M&E officers",
  "administrative_staff"                                     = "Admin staff",
  "program_coordinators"                                     = "Program coordinators",
  "training_coordinators_facilitators"                       = "Training coordinators",
  "agroforestry_extension_officers"                          = "Agroforestry extension",
  "nursery_managers"                                         = "Nursery managers",
  "nursery_workers"                                          = "Nursery workers",
  "technical_support_roles_e_g_soil_scientists_agronomists"  = "Technical support",
  "eco_tourism_operators_e_g_tour_guides_lodge_staff"        = "Eco-tourism operators",
  "cultural_heritage_and_craft_trainers"                     = "Cultural heritage & craft",
  "craftsmen_and_woodcarvers"                                = "Craftsmen & woodcarvers",
  "processing_plant_staff_e_g_honey_timber_charcoal"         = "Processing natural resources",
  "marketing_and_sales_agents"                               = "Marketing & sales",
  "researchers"                                              = "Researchers",
  "data_analysts"                                            = "Data analysts",
  "surveyors"                                                = "Surveyors",
  "field_data_collectors"                                    = "Field data collectors",
  "biodiversity_monitors"                                    = "Biodiversity monitors",
  "forest_extension_workers"                                 = "Forest extension workers",
  "forest_protection_and_fire_mnagement"                     = "Forest protection & fire",
  "enterprise_and_processing_roles"                          = "Enterprise",
  "training_and_facilitation"                                = "Training & facilitation",
  "security_services"                                        = "Security services",
  "livestock_and_resource_management"                        = "Livestock & resource mgmt",
  "interns_trainees"                                         = "Interns & trainees",
  "technical_extension_roles"                                = "Technical extension"
)

full_var_names <- paste0(prefix, names(job_labels))

binary_freq <- cfmg |>
  filter(interview_type %in% c("cfmg_female", "cfmg_male")) |>
  select(all_of(full_var_names)) |>
  summarise(across(everything(), ~ sum(. == 1, na.rm = TRUE))) |>
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "variable", values_to = "freq") |>
  mutate(word = job_labels[sub(prefix, "", variable)]) |>
  filter(freq > 0) |>
  select(word, freq)

comment_freq <- cfmg |>
  filter(interview_type %in% c("cfmg_female", "cfmg_male")) |>
  select(text = economic_transformation_formal_employment_opportunities_from_cfm_comment) |>
  filter(!is.na(text), trimws(text) != "") |>
  unnest_tokens(word, text) |>
  anti_join(stop_words, by = "word") |>
  mutate(word = case_match(word,
    "hammer" ~ "Hammer mill",
    "mill"   ~ "Hammer mill",
    "cows"   ~ "Cattle",
    .default = word
  )) |>
  count(word, name = "freq")

wc_data <- bind_rows(binary_freq, comment_freq) |>
  group_by(word) |>
  summarise(freq = sum(freq), .groups = "drop")

set.seed(42)
ggplot(wc_data, aes(label = word, size = freq, colour = freq)) +
  geom_text_wordcloud(eccentricity = 0.65, seed = 42) +
  scale_size(range = c(3, 30)) +
  scale_colour_gradient(low = "#6ABFB8", high = "#1A6B63") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(plot.margin = margin(0, 0, 0, 0))
```

### Income earning opportunities
#### Carbon finance
Executive FGDs reported whether their CFMG was involved in carbon finance (@fig-et_carbon_finance).

```{r}
#| label: fig-et_carbon_finance
#| code-fold: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| fig-cap: "CFMG involvement in carbon finance by province, as reported by executive FGDs."
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 6

cfmg |>
  filter(interview_type == "cfmg_executive") |>
  transmute(
    province,
    response = case_when(
      economic_transformation_cfma_involved_carbon_finance == "yes"                          ~ "Yes",
      str_starts(economic_transformation_cfma_involved_carbon_finance, "Yes")                ~ "Yes",
      economic_transformation_cfma_involved_carbon_finance == "no"                           ~ "No",
      str_starts(economic_transformation_cfma_involved_carbon_finance, "No")                 ~ "No",
      economic_transformation_cfma_involved_carbon_finance %in% c(
        "Not yet", "At the moment_they are not",
        "The cfmg is yet to enter into partnership with the carbon credit organization",
        "The CFMG has not signed form 7 and MOU. They had to cancel the one they had signed earlier due to disagreements in the benefits sharing mechanism for income from carbon credits",
        "Has not signed the agreement with CCS yet due to dispute  over percentages in the benefit sharing mechanism"
      ) ~ "No",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  ) |>
  drop_na(response) |>
  mutate(response = factor(response, levels = c("Yes", "No"))) |>
  count(province, response) |>
  mutate(n_signed = if_else(response == "No", -n, n)) |>
  ggplot(aes(y = reorder(province, n_signed), x = n_signed, fill = response)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.65, colour = "white", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, colour = "white", linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label  = n,
        hjust  = if_else(response == "No", 0.4, -0.4),
        colour = response),
    size = 4, fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("No" = "white", "Yes" = "grey25"),
                      guide = "none") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("No" = "#E07B54", "Yes" = "#2A9D8F"),
                    guide  = guide_legend(reverse = TRUE)) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = ~abs(.x),
                     expand = expansion(add = 1.5),
                     name   = "Number of CFMGs") +
  scale_y_discrete(expand = expansion(add = 0.5)) +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
  labs(y = NULL, fill = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 18) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    axis.line.x.bottom = element_line(colour = "grey30", linewidth = 0.5),
    axis.text          = element_text(colour = "grey20"),
    legend.position    = "bottom",
    plot.margin        = margin(10, 25, 10, 10)
  )
```

FGD groups were also asked whether the community was aware of the carbon credit agreement (@fig-et_carbon_awareness).

```{r}
#| label: fig-et_carbon_awareness
#| code-fold: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| fig-cap: "Community awareness of the carbon credit agreement by FGD group."
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 5

cfmg |>
  filter(interview_type %in% c("cfmg_executive", "cfmg_female",
                                "cfmg_male", "cfmg_youth")) |>
  transmute(
    group = factor(
      case_match(
        interview_type,
        "cfmg_executive" ~ "Executive",
        "cfmg_female"    ~ "Female",
        "cfmg_male"      ~ "Male",
        "cfmg_youth"     ~ "Youth"
      ),
      levels = c("Youth", "Male", "Female", "Executive")
    ),
    response = case_when(
      economic_transformation_community_awareness_carbon_credit_agreement == "yes" ~ "Yes",
      economic_transformation_community_awareness_carbon_credit_agreement == "no"  ~ "No",
      economic_transformation_community_awareness_carbon_credit_agreement %in% c(
        "Not aware of the agreement of the Juristiction project we just know what's involved",
        "The agreement is not clear we don't know where they sale the carbon and we don't know how much they get from the money",
        "So far we have just heard the chief signed"
      ) ~ "No",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  ) |>
  drop_na(response) |>
  mutate(response = factor(response, levels = c("Yes", "No"))) |>
  count(group, response) |>
  mutate(n_signed = if_else(response == "No", -n, n)) |>
  ggplot(aes(y = group, x = n_signed, fill = response)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.65, colour = "white", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, colour = "white", linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label  = n,
        hjust  = if_else(response == "No", 0.4, -0.4),
        colour = response),
    size = 4, fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("No" = "white", "Yes" = "grey25"),
                      guide = "none") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("No" = "#E07B54", "Yes" = "#2A9D8F"),
                    guide  = guide_legend(reverse = TRUE)) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = ~abs(.x),
                     expand = expansion(add = 3),
                     name   = "Number of FGDs") +
  scale_y_discrete(expand = expansion(add = 0.5)) +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
  labs(y = NULL, fill = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 18) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    axis.line.x.bottom = element_line(colour = "grey30", linewidth = 0.5),
    axis.text          = element_text(colour = "grey20"),
    legend.position    = "bottom",
    plot.margin        = margin(10, 25, 10, 10)
  )
```

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
### Carbon finance awareness – FGDs – Model methods
To examine differences among FGD types in whether they reported community awareness of the carbon credit agreement, we employed a generalised linear mixed model (GLMM) with a binomial response and random intercepts for province and CFMG nested within province. We used FGD type as the factor. We examined the significance of pairwise differences among FGD types using Tukey HSD.
:::

```{r}
#| label: model_et_fgd_carbon_awareness
#| code-fold: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false

test.data.ca <- cfmg |>
  filter(interview_type %in% c("cfmg_executive", "cfmg_female",
                                "cfmg_male", "cfmg_youth")) |>
  transmute(
    province, cfmg_name,
    interview_type = factor(
      case_match(
        interview_type,
        "cfmg_executive" ~ "Executive",
        "cfmg_female"    ~ "Female",
        "cfmg_male"      ~ "Male",
        "cfmg_youth"     ~ "Youth"
      ),
      levels = c("Executive", "Female", "Male", "Youth")
    ),
    empl = case_when(
      economic_transformation_community_awareness_carbon_credit_agreement == "yes" ~ 1L,
      economic_transformation_community_awareness_carbon_credit_agreement == "no"  ~ 0L,
      economic_transformation_community_awareness_carbon_credit_agreement %in% c(
        "Not aware of the agreement of the Juristiction project we just know what's involved",
        "The agreement is not clear we don't know where they sale the carbon and we don't know how much they get from the money",
        "So far we have just heard the chief signed"
      ) ~ 0L,
      TRUE ~ NA_integer_
    )
  ) |>
  drop_na(empl) |>
  mutate(province_cfmg = interaction(province, cfmg_name))

M11 <- glmer(empl ~ interview_type + (1 | province) + (1 | province_cfmg),
            data = test.data.ca, family = binomial)

emm.M11      <- emmeans(M11, specs = ~ interview_type, type = "response")
pairwise.M11 <- pairs(emm.M11, adjust = "Tukey")
```

```{r}
#| label: M11_et_fgd_carbon_awareness
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| output-fold: true
#| output-summary: "Carbon finance awareness – FGD model results (click to expand)"

summary(M11)
```

```{r}
#| label: pairwise_et_fgd_carbon_awareness
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| output-fold: true
#| output-summary: "Carbon finance awareness – FGD pairwise comparisons (click to expand)"

summary(pairwise.M11)
```

Individual interviewees were also asked whether the community was aware of the carbon credit agreement. @tbl-et_individual_carbon_awareness shows the percentage reporting awareness, broken down by interviewee role.

```{r}
#| label: tbl-et_individual_carbon_awareness
#| code-fold: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| tbl-cap: "Percentage of individual interviewees reporting community awareness of the carbon credit agreement, by interviewee role."

cfmg |>
  filter(interview_type == "individual_interview") |>
  select(
    interviewee_role,
    empl = economic_transformation_community_awareness_carbon_credit_agreement
  ) |>
  mutate(
    interviewee_role = case_match(
      as.character(interviewee_role),
      "cfmg_member"      ~ "Community member",
      "cfmg_exec_member" ~ "CFMG executive member",
      "trad_leader"      ~ "Traditional leader",
      "dfo"              ~ "District Forestry Officer",
      "NGO_rep"          ~ "NGO representative"
    ),
    interviewee_role = factor(interviewee_role, levels = c(
      "Community member", "CFMG executive member", "Traditional leader",
      "District Forestry Officer", "NGO representative"
    )),
    empl = case_match(empl,
                      "yes" ~ 1,
                      "no"  ~ 0,
                      .default = NA)
  ) |>
  group_by(interviewee_role) |>
  summarise(
    n         = n(),
    Awareness = mean(empl, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups   = "drop"
  ) |>
  arrange(interviewee_role) |>
  gt() |>
  cols_label(
    interviewee_role = "Role",
    n                = "n",
    Awareness        = "Aware of carbon credit agreement"
  ) |>
  fmt_percent(
    columns  = Awareness,
    decimals = 0
  ) |>
  tab_header(
    title    = "Community awareness of the carbon credit agreement, as reported by individual interviewees",
    subtitle = "Values represent the % of positive responses. n = the total number of interview responses."
  ) |>
  opt_align_table_header(align = "left") |>
  cols_align(align = "left",   columns = interviewee_role) |>
  cols_align(align = "center", columns = c(n, Awareness)) |>
  tab_style(
    style     = cell_text(align = "center"),
    locations = cells_column_labels(columns = interviewee_role)
  ) |>
  sub_missing()
```

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
### Carbon finance awareness – Individual interviewees – Model methods
To examine differences among individual interviewee roles in whether they reported community awareness of the carbon credit agreement, we employed a generalised linear mixed model (GLMM) with a binomial response and random intercepts for province and CFMG nested within province. We included interviewee role, interviewee gender and interviewee age as factors. To examine pairwise differences among interviewee roles, we employed Tukey HSD.
:::

```{r}
#| label: model_et_individual_carbon_awareness
#| code-fold: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false

test.data.ca.ind <- cfmg |>
  filter(interview_type == "individual_interview") |>
  mutate(
    empl = case_match(
      economic_transformation_community_awareness_carbon_credit_agreement,
      "yes" ~ 1,
      "no"  ~ 0,
      .default = NA
    ),
    interviewee_gender = demo_information_interviewee_gender,
    interviewee_age    = demo_information_interviewee_age
  ) |>
  drop_na(empl) |>
  mutate(province_cfmg = interaction(province, cfmg_name))

M12 <- glmer(empl ~ interviewee_role + interviewee_gender + interviewee_age +
              (1 | province) + (1 | province_cfmg),
            data = test.data.ca.ind, family = binomial)

emm.M12      <- emmeans(M12, specs = ~ interviewee_role, type = "response")
pairwise.M12 <- pairs(emm.M12, adjust = "Tukey")
```

```{r}
#| label: M12_et_individual_carbon_awareness
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| output-fold: true
#| output-summary: "Carbon finance awareness – Individual model results (click to expand)"

summary(M12)
```

```{r}
#| label: pairwise_et_individual_carbon_awareness
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| output-fold: true
#| output-summary: "Carbon finance awareness – Individual pairwise comparisons (click to expand)"

summary(pairwise.M12)
```


#### Carbon trader support
The types of CFM activities supported by the carbon trader are shown in @fig-et_trader_support.

```{r}
#| label: fig-et_trader_support
#| code-fold: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| fig-cap: "Types of CFM activities supported by the carbon trader."
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 5

prefix_ts <- "economic_transformation_cfm_activities_supported_by_carbon_trader_"

ts_labels <- c(
  "patrolling_and_monitoring"                        = "Patrolling",
  "fire_management"                                  = "Fire management",
  "biodiversity_conservation"                        = "Biodiversity",
  "agroforestry"                                     = "Agroforestry",
  "climate_smart_farming"                            = "Climate-smart farming",
  "non_timber_forest_products_ntfps"                 = "NTFPs",
  "eco_tourism"                                      = "Eco-tourism",
  "tree_planting"                                    = "Tree planting",
  "training_and_capacity_building"                   = "Training",
  "carbon_stock_tracking"                            = "Carbon tracking",
  "market"                                           = "Market access",
  "alternative_livelihood_activities"                = "Livelihoods",
  "infractrure"                                      = "Infrastructure",
  "social_gatherings"                                = "Social gatherings",
  "employment"                                       = "Employment",
  "community_sensitization_awareness_engangement"    = "Sensitisation"
)

full_ts_vars <- paste0(prefix_ts, names(ts_labels))

ts_freq <- cfmg |>
  select(all_of(full_ts_vars)) |>
  summarise(across(everything(), ~ sum(. == 1, na.rm = TRUE))) |>
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "variable", values_to = "freq") |>
  mutate(word = ts_labels[sub(prefix_ts, "", variable, fixed = TRUE)]) |>
  filter(freq > 0) |>
  select(word, freq)

set.seed(42)
ggplot(ts_freq, aes(label = word, size = freq, colour = freq)) +
  geom_text_wordcloud(eccentricity = 0.65, seed = 42) +
  scale_size(range = c(3, 18)) +
  scale_colour_gradient(low = "#6ABFB8", high = "#1A6B63") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(plot.margin = margin(0, 0, 0, 0))
```

#### Other income earning opportunities
FGD groups also reported whether CFM had generated specific income earning opportunities (@fig-et_income_opportunities).

```{r}
#| label: fig-et_income_opportunities
#| code-fold: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| fig-cap: "Income opportunities reported as economic benefits of CFM as reported by female and male FGDs. The length of the bars represents the number of FGDs reporting a benefit (Yes) or no benefit (No) for each income source."
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 5

cfmg |>
  filter(interview_type %in% c("cfmg_female", "cfmg_male")) |>
  mutate(interview_type = case_match(
    interview_type,
    "cfmg_female" ~ "Female FGD",
    "cfmg_male"   ~ "Male FGD"
  )) |>
  select(
    interview_type,
    "Ecotourism income"         = economic_transformation_economic_benefits_from_cfm_income_from_ecotourism_e_g_guided_tours_camping_fees,
    "Forest product processing" = economic_transformation_economic_benefits_from_cfm_income_from_forest_product_processing_e_g_honey_bamboo,
    "Conservation revenue"      = economic_transformation_economic_benefits_from_cfm_revenue_from_forest_conservation_programs_e_g_carbon_credits,
    "Alternative livelihoods"   = `economic_transformation_economic_benefits_from_cfm_alternative-livelihoods_local_business_development_e_g_forest_based_crafts_small_scale_industries`,
    "Market linkages"           = economic_transformation_economic_benefits_from_cfm_market_linkages_and_trade_opportunities_for_forest_products
  ) |>
  pivot_longer(-interview_type, names_to = "income_source", values_to = "value") |>
  filter(!is.na(value)) |>
  mutate(
    response = case_match(as.character(value), "1" ~ "Yes", "0" ~ "No"),
    response = factor(response, levels = c("No", "Yes")),
    income_source = factor(income_source, levels = c(
      "Ecotourism income", "Forest product processing", "Conservation revenue",
      "Alternative livelihoods", "Market linkages"
    )),
    interview_type = factor(interview_type, levels = c("Female FGD", "Male FGD"))
  ) |>
  count(interview_type, income_source, response) |>
  mutate(n_signed = if_else(response == "No", -n, n)) |>
  (\(d) ggplot(d, aes(y = income_source, x = n_signed, fill = response)) +
    geom_blank(data = tibble(
      interview_type = factor(
        c("Female FGD", "Female FGD", "Male FGD", "Male FGD"),
        levels = c("Female FGD", "Male FGD")
      ),
      income_source = factor(
        rep("Ecotourism income", 4),
        levels = c("Ecotourism income", "Forest product processing",
                   "Conservation revenue", "Alternative livelihoods", "Market linkages")
      ),
      n_signed = c(-60, 0, -60, 10),
      response = factor(c("No", "Yes", "No", "Yes"), levels = c("No", "Yes"))
    )) +
    geom_col(width = 0.65, colour = "white", linewidth = 0.4) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, colour = "white", linewidth = 1.2) +
    geom_text(
      aes(label  = n,
          hjust  = if_else(response == "No", -0.4, -0.4),
          colour = response),
      size = 3, fontface = "bold"
    ) +
    scale_colour_manual(
      values = c("No" = "white", "Yes" = "grey25"),
      guide  = "none"
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(
      labels = ~abs(.x),
      breaks = seq(-60, 10, by = 20),
      expand = expansion(add = 0),
      name   = "Number of FGDs"
    ) +
    coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
    scale_y_discrete(expand = expansion(add = 0.5)) +
    scale_fill_manual(
      values = c("No" = "#E07B54", "Yes" = "#2A9D8F"),
      guide  = guide_legend(reverse = FALSE)
    ) +
    facet_wrap(~ interview_type, scales = "free_x") +
    labs(y = NULL, fill = NULL) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 18) +
    theme(
      panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
      axis.line.x.bottom = element_line(colour = "grey30", linewidth = 0.5),
      axis.text.y        = element_text(size = rel(1.14), colour = "grey20"),
      axis.text.x        = element_text(size = rel(1.14), colour = "grey20"),
      axis.title.x       = element_text(size = rel(1), colour = "grey40"),
      legend.position    = "bottom",
      legend.text        = element_text(size = rel(1)),
      strip.text         = element_text(size = rel(1.14), face = "bold"),
      plot.margin        = margin(10, 25, 10, 10)
    )
  )()
```

Female and male FGDs also identified additional sources of income generated by CFM (@fig-et_income_sources).

```{r}
#| label: fig-et_income_sources
#| code-fold: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| fig-cap: "Additional sources of income from CFM reported by female and male FGDs. Word size is proportional to the number of FGDs reporting each income source."
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 7

prefix_income <- "economic_transformatioNAdditional_sources_of_income_additional_income_sources_from_cfm_"

income_freq <- cfmg |>
  filter(interview_type %in% c("cfmg_female", "cfmg_male")) |>
  select(starts_with(prefix_income)) |>
  summarise(across(everything(), ~ sum(. == 1, na.rm = TRUE))) |>
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "variable", values_to = "freq") |>
  mutate(
    word = variable |>
      sub(prefix_income, "", x = _, fixed = TRUE) |>
      gsub("_", " ", x = _) |>
      str_to_title(),
    word = case_match(word,
      "Community Business And Enterprise" ~ "Community business",
      "Wildlife Based And Safari Income"  ~ "Wildlife based",
      .default = word
    )
  ) |>
  filter(
    freq > 0,
    !str_starts(word, "Rank"),
    !word %in% c("None", "Dont Know")
  ) |>
  select(word, freq)

set.seed(42)
ggplot(income_freq, aes(label = word, size = freq, colour = freq)) +
  geom_text_wordcloud(eccentricity = 0.65, seed = 42) +
  scale_size(range = c(3, 22)) +
  scale_colour_gradient(low = "#6ABFB8", high = "#1A6B63") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(plot.margin = margin(0, 0, 0, 0))
```

Female and male FGDs also ranked their top three additional sources of income from CFM (@fig-et_income_ranks).

```{r}
#| label: fig-et_income_ranks
#| code-fold: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| fig-cap: "Ranking of additional income sources from CFM by female and male FGDs. Bars show the number of FGDs assigning each rank. Only income sources receiving at least one top-three ranking are shown."
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 7

cfmg |>
  filter(interview_type %in% c("cfmg_female", "cfmg_male")) |>
  select(matches("Additional_sources_of_income.*rank_")) |>
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "variable", values_to = "rank_val") |>
  mutate(
    rank_val = as.numeric(rank_val),
    source   = sub(".*rank_", "", variable) |>
      gsub("_", " ", x = _) |>
      str_to_title(),
    source = case_match(source,
      "Wildlife Based And Safari Income"  ~ "Wildlife Based",
      "Community Business And Enterprise" ~ "Community Business",
      .default = source
    )
  ) |>
  filter(rank_val %in% c(1, 2, 3), source != "Carbon Credits") |>
  mutate(rank = factor(paste0("Rank ", rank_val), levels = c("Rank 3", "Rank 2", "Rank 1"))) |>
  count(source, rank) |>
  group_by(source) |>
  mutate(total = sum(n)) |>
  ungroup() |>
  ggplot(aes(y = reorder(source, total), x = n, fill = rank)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7, colour = "white", linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = n),
    position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5),
    size = 3.5, colour = "white", fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.05)),
    name   = "Number of FGDs"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Rank 1" = "#264653",
      "Rank 2" = "#E9C46A",
      "Rank 3" = "#E07B54"
    ),
    guide = guide_legend(reverse = TRUE)
  ) +
  labs(y = NULL, fill = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 18) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_line(colour = "grey85", linewidth = 0.4),
    axis.line.x.bottom = element_line(colour = "grey30", linewidth = 0.5),
    axis.text.y        = element_text(size = rel(1.14), colour = "grey20"),
    axis.text.x        = element_text(size = rel(1.14), colour = "grey20"),
    axis.title.x       = element_text(size = rel(1), colour = "grey40"),
    legend.position    = "bottom",
    legend.text        = element_text(size = rel(1)),
    plot.margin        = margin(10, 25, 10, 10)
  )
```

Executive FGDs also reported the estimated annual income generated by the CFMG from CFM activities (@fig-et_annual_income).

```{r}
#| label: fig-et_annual_income
#| code-fold: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| fig-cap: "Mean estimated annual income from CFM by province, as reported by executive FGDs."
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 5

cfmg |>
  filter(interview_type == "cfmg_executive") |>
  select(
    province,
    income = economic_transformation_estimated_annual_income_from_cfm
  ) |>
  filter(!is.na(income), income <= 250000) |>
  mutate(province = reorder(province, income, FUN = median)) |>
  ggplot(aes(y = province, x = income)) +
  geom_boxplot(
    width = 0.5, fill = "grey92", colour = "grey40",
    outlier.shape = NA
  ) +
  geom_jitter(
    height = 0.15, width = 0,
    size = 2, alpha = 0.7, colour = "#2A9D8F"
  ) +
  stat_summary(
    fun = mean, geom = "point",
    colour = "red", size = 3, shape = 16
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = label_comma(),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.1)),
    name   = "Estimated annual income from CFM"
  ) +
  labs(y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 18) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_line(colour = "grey85", linewidth = 0.4),
    axis.line.x.bottom = element_line(colour = "grey30", linewidth = 0.5),
    axis.text.y        = element_text(size = rel(1.14), colour = "grey20"),
    axis.text.x        = element_text(size = rel(1.14), colour = "grey20"),
    axis.title.x       = element_text(size = rel(1), colour = "grey40"),
    plot.margin        = margin(10, 25, 10, 10)
  )
```

Female and male FGDs also reported which forest products were harvested for household use (@fig-et_household_products).

```{r}
#| label: fig-et_household_products
#| code-fold: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| fig-cap: "Forest products harvested for household use reported by female and male FGDs. Word size is proportional to the number of FGDs reporting each product."
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 7

prefix_hh <- "economic_transformation_products_harvested_household_use_"

hh_freq <- cfmg |>
  filter(interview_type %in% c("cfmg_female", "cfmg_male")) |>
  select(starts_with(prefix_hh)) |>
  summarise(across(everything(), ~ sum(. == 1, na.rm = TRUE))) |>
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "variable", values_to = "freq") |>
  mutate(
    word = variable |>
      sub(prefix_hh, "", x = _, fixed = TRUE) |>
      gsub("_", " ", x = _) |>
      str_to_title()
  ) |>
  filter(
    freq > 0,
    !word %in% c("Carbon Credits", "Dont Know", "None")
  ) |>
  select(word, freq)

set.seed(42)
ggplot(hh_freq, aes(label = word, size = freq, colour = freq)) +
  geom_text_wordcloud(eccentricity = 0.65, seed = 42) +
  scale_size(range = c(3, 22)) +
  scale_colour_gradient(low = "#6ABFB8", high = "#1A6B63") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(plot.margin = margin(0, 0, 0, 0))
```

Executive FGDs were asked whether the CFMG had collaborated with private enterprise (@fig-et_collaboration).

```{r}
#| label: fig-et_collaboration
#| code-fold: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| fig-cap: "CFMG collaboration with private enterprise, as reported by executive FGDs by province."
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 6

collab_theme <- list(
  theme_minimal(base_size = 18),
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    axis.line.x.bottom = element_line(colour = "grey30", linewidth = 0.5),
    axis.text.y        = element_text(size = rel(1.14), colour = "grey20"),
    axis.text.x        = element_text(size = rel(1.14), colour = "grey20"),
    axis.title.x       = element_text(size = rel(1), colour = "grey40"),
    legend.position    = "bottom",
    legend.text        = element_text(size = rel(1)),
    plot.title         = element_text(size = rel(1.14), face = "bold"),
    plot.margin        = margin(10, 25, 10, 10)
  )
)

# Plot 1: private enterprise collaboration (Yes / No)
p1 <- cfmg |>
  filter(interview_type == "cfmg_executive") |>
  transmute(
    province,
    response = case_match(
      economic_transformation_cfmg_private_enterprise_collaboration,
      "yes" ~ "Yes", "no" ~ "No"
    )
  ) |>
  drop_na(response) |>
  mutate(response = factor(response, levels = c("Yes", "No"))) |>
  count(province, response) |>
  mutate(n_signed = if_else(response == "No", -n, n)) |>
  ggplot(aes(y = reorder(province, n_signed), x = n_signed, fill = response)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.65, colour = "white", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, colour = "white", linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label  = n,
        hjust  = if_else(response == "No", 0.4, -0.4),
        colour = response),
    size = 3.5, fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("No" = "white", "Yes" = "grey25"), guide = "none") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = ~abs(.x), expand = expansion(add = 1),
                     name = "Number of CFMGs") +
  scale_y_discrete(expand = expansion(add = 0.5)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("No" = "#E07B54", "Yes" = "#2A9D8F")) +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
  labs(y = NULL, fill = NULL,
       title = "CFMG-private enterprise collaboration") +
  collab_theme

p1
```

The value chains that CFMGs reported working with through private enterprise partnerships are shown in @fig-et_value_chains.

```{r}
#| label: fig-et_value_chains
#| code-fold: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| fig-cap: "Value chains that CFMGs collaborate with through private enterprise partnerships, as reported by executive FGDs."
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 5

prefix_vc <- "economic_transformation_private_enterprises_cfmg_works_with_"

vc_labels <- c(
  "timber_companies"               = "Timber",
  "sawmills"                       = "Sawmills",
  "furniture_manufacturers"        = "Furniture",
  "wood_processing_enterprises"    = "Wood processing",
  "eco_tourism_operators"          = "Eco-tourism",
  "mushroom_processors_traders"    = "Mushrooms",
  "wild_fruit_processors_traders"  = "Wild fruit",
  "insect_processors_traders"      = "Insects",
  "chikanda_processors_traders"    = "Chikanda",
  "carbon_credit_traders"          = "Carbon credits",
  "beekeeping_and_honey_producers" = "Honey",
  "charcoal"                       = "Charcoal",
  "botanicals_harvesting"          = "Botanicals"
)

full_vc_vars <- paste0(prefix_vc, names(vc_labels))

vc_freq <- cfmg |>
  filter(interview_type == "cfmg_executive") |>
  select(all_of(full_vc_vars)) |>
  summarise(across(everything(), ~ sum(. == 1, na.rm = TRUE))) |>
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "variable", values_to = "freq") |>
  mutate(label = vc_labels[sub(prefix_vc, "", variable, fixed = TRUE)]) |>
  filter(freq > 0) |>
  select(label, freq)

ggplot(vc_freq, aes(x = freq, y = reorder(label, freq))) +
  geom_col(fill = "#2A9D8F", width = 0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = freq), hjust = -0.3, size = 4.5, colour = "grey30") +
  scale_x_continuous(
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15)),
    name   = "Number of CFMGs"
  ) +
  scale_y_discrete(expand = expansion(add = 0.5)) +
  labs(y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 18) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    axis.line.x.bottom = element_line(colour = "grey30", linewidth = 0.5),
    axis.text          = element_text(colour = "grey20"),
    plot.margin        = margin(10, 25, 10, 10)
  )
```

FGD groups also identified economic opportunities that remained unrealised within their CFMGs (@fig-et_unrealised).

```{r}
#| label: fig-et_unrealised
#| code-fold: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| fig-cap: "Unrealised economic opportunities identified by FGD groups."
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 5

prefix_ur <- "economic_transformation_economic_oppportunities_unrealized_economic_opportunities_"

ur_labels <- c(
  "timber"                    = "Timber",
  "firewood"                  = "Firewood",
  "charcoal"                  = "Charcoal",
  "honey"                     = "Honey",
  "mushrooms"                 = "Mushrooms",
  "wild_fruits"               = "Wild fruits",
  "caterpillars"              = "Caterpillars",
  "chikanda"                  = "Chikanda",
  "munkoyo"                   = "Munkoyo",
  "lusala_busala"             = "Lusala/Busala",
  "mungongo"                  = "Mungongo",
  "water"                     = "Water",
  "devils_claw"               = "Devil's claw",
  "fish"                      = "Fish",
  "wildlife"                  = "Wildlife",
  "minerals_mining"           = "Minerals",
  "clay"                      = "Clay",
  "sand"                      = "Sand",
  "grass"                     = "Grass",
  "livestock"                 = "Livestock",
  "crops"                     = "Crops",
  "tourism_education_tours"   = "Tourism",
  "permitting"                = "Permitting",
  "infrastruture_utilities"   = "Infrastructure",
  "bamboo"                    = "Bamboo",
  "medicinal_plants"          = "Medicinal plants",
  "bushmeat"                  = "Bushmeat",
  "resins_gums"               = "Resins",
  "thatching_grass"           = "Thatching grass",
  "fibers"                    = "Fibres",
  "eco_tourism"               = "Eco-tourism"
)

full_ur_vars <- paste0(prefix_ur, names(ur_labels))

ur_freq <- cfmg |>
  filter(interview_type %in% c("cfmg_female", "cfmg_male")) |>
  select(all_of(full_ur_vars)) |>
  summarise(across(everything(), ~ sum(. == 1, na.rm = TRUE))) |>
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "variable", values_to = "freq") |>
  mutate(word = ur_labels[sub(prefix_ur, "", variable, fixed = TRUE)]) |>
  filter(freq > 0) |>
  select(word, freq)

set.seed(42)
ggplot(ur_freq, aes(label = word, size = freq, colour = freq)) +
  geom_text_wordcloud(eccentricity = 0.65, seed = 42) +
  scale_size(range = c(3, 22)) +
  scale_colour_gradient(low = "#6ABFB8", high = "#1A6B63") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(plot.margin = margin(0, 0, 0, 0))
```

The ranking of those unrealised opportunities is shown in @fig-et_unrealised_ranks.

```{r}
#| label: fig-et_unrealised_ranks
#| code-fold: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| fig-cap: "Ranking of unrealised economic opportunities by FGD groups. Bars show the frequency of rank 1 (highest priority), rank 2, and rank 3 responses."
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 7

prefix_ur_r <- "economic_transformation_economic_oppportunities_rank_"

ur_rank_labels <- c(
  "timber"                    = "Timber",
  "firewood"                  = "Firewood",
  "charcoal"                  = "Charcoal",
  "honey"                     = "Honey",
  "mushrooms"                 = "Mushrooms",
  "wild_fruits"               = "Wild fruits",
  "caterpillars"              = "Caterpillars",
  "chikanda"                  = "Chikanda",
  "munkoyo"                   = "Munkoyo",
  "lusala_busala"             = "Lusala/Busala",
  "mungongo"                  = "Mungongo",
  "water"                     = "Water",
  "devils_claw"               = "Devil's claw",
  "fish"                      = "Fish",
  "wildlife"                  = "Wildlife",
  "minerals_mining"           = "Minerals",
  "clay"                      = "Clay",
  "sand"                      = "Sand",
  "grass"                     = "Grass",
  "livestock"                 = "Livestock",
  "crops"                     = "Crops",
  "tourism_education_tours"   = "Tourism",
  "permitting"                = "Permitting",
  "bamboo"                    = "Bamboo",
  "medicinal_plants"          = "Medicinal plants",
  "bushmeat"                  = "Bushmeat",
  "resins_gums"               = "Resins",
  "thatching_grass"           = "Thatching grass",
  "fibers"                    = "Fibres",
  "eco_tourism"               = "Eco-tourism"
)

full_ur_rank_vars <- paste0(prefix_ur_r, names(ur_rank_labels))

cfmg |>
  filter(interview_type %in% c("cfmg_female", "cfmg_male")) |>
  select(all_of(full_ur_rank_vars)) |>
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "variable", values_to = "rank_val") |>
  mutate(
    rank_val = as.numeric(rank_val),
    source   = ur_rank_labels[sub(prefix_ur_r, "", variable, fixed = TRUE)]
  ) |>
  filter(rank_val %in% c(1, 2, 3)) |>
  mutate(rank = factor(paste0("Rank ", rank_val), levels = c("Rank 3", "Rank 2", "Rank 1"))) |>
  count(source, rank) |>
  group_by(source) |>
  mutate(total = sum(n)) |>
  ungroup() |>
  ggplot(aes(y = reorder(source, total), x = n, fill = rank)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7, colour = "white", linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = n),
    position  = position_stack(vjust = 0.5),
    size      = 3.5, colour = "white", fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("Rank 1" = "#264653", "Rank 2" = "#E9C46A", "Rank 3" = "#E07B54"),
    guide  = guide_legend(reverse = TRUE)
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.05)),
    name   = "Number of responses"
  ) +
  scale_y_discrete(expand = expansion(add = 0.5)) +
  labs(y = NULL, fill = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 18) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    axis.line.x.bottom = element_line(colour = "grey30", linewidth = 0.5),
    axis.text          = element_text(colour = "grey20"),
    legend.position    = "bottom",
    plot.margin        = margin(10, 15, 10, 10)
  )
```

FGD groups also identified potential value-added opportunities from CFM resources (@fig-et_value_added).

```{r}
#| label: fig-et_value_added
#| code-fold: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| fig-cap: "Potential value-added opportunities identified by FGD groups."
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 5

prefix_va <- "economic_transformation_potential_value_added_opportunities_"

va_labels <- c(
  "timber_processing_e_g_furniture_carpentry_woodcrafts"                                                    = "Timber processing",
  "charcoal_briquetting_eco_friendly_alternatives_to_traditional_charcoal"                                  = "Charcoal briquettes",
  "honey_processing_and_packaging_e_g_branded_honey_beeswax_products"                                      = "Honey processing",
  "mushroom_drying_and_packaging_for_extended_shelf_life_and_market_access"                                 = "Dried mushrooms",
  "wild_fruit_processing_e_g_juices_jams_dried_fruits"                                                     = "Wild fruits",
  "caterpillar_preservation_and_packaging_e_g_dried_or_canned_for_sale"                                    = "Caterpillars",
  "chikanda"                                                                                                 = "Chikanda",
  "munkoyo"                                                                                                  = "Munkoyo",
  "lusala_busala"                                                                                            = "Lusala/Busala",
  "mungongo"                                                                                                 = "Mungongo",
  "devils_claw"                                                                                              = "Devil's claw",
  "palm_leaves"                                                                                              = "Palm leaves",
  "grass"                                                                                                    = "Grass",
  "fish"                                                                                                     = "Fish",
  "minerals"                                                                                                 = "Minerals",
  "bamboo_products_e_g_furniture_handicrafts_construction_materials"                                        = "Bamboo products",
  "medicinal_plant_extraction_e_g_herbal_teas_essential_oils_medicinal_creams"                              = "Medicinal plants",
  "handicrafts_and_woven_products_e_g_baskets_mats_textiles"                                                = "Handicrafts",
  "resin_and_gum_processing_e_g_adhesives_fragrances"                                                       = "Resins",
  "ecotourism_development_e_g_eco_lodges_cultural_experiences_tour_guiding"                                 = "Eco-tourism",
  "agroforestry_based_businesses_e_g_tree_nurseries_sustainable_agriculture"                                = "Agroforestry"
)

full_va_vars <- paste0(prefix_va, names(va_labels))

va_freq <- cfmg |>
  filter(interview_type %in% c("cfmg_female", "cfmg_male")) |>
  select(all_of(full_va_vars)) |>
  summarise(across(everything(), ~ sum(. == 1, na.rm = TRUE))) |>
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "variable", values_to = "freq") |>
  mutate(word = va_labels[sub(prefix_va, "", variable, fixed = TRUE)]) |>
  filter(freq > 0) |>
  select(word, freq)

set.seed(42)
ggplot(va_freq, aes(label = word, size = freq, colour = freq)) +
  geom_text_wordcloud(eccentricity = 0.65, seed = 42) +
  scale_size(range = c(3, 22)) +
  scale_colour_gradient(low = "#6ABFB8", high = "#1A6B63") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(plot.margin = margin(0, 0, 0, 0))
```

Executive FGDs reported which primary markets CFM products are sold into (@fig-et_markets).

```{r}
#| label: fig-et_markets
#| code-fold: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| fig-cap: "Primary markets for CFM products, as reported by executive FGDs."
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 4

market_labels <- c(
  "economic_transformation_cfm_products_primary_market_local_markets"    = "Local",
  "economic_transformation_cfm_products_primary_market_national_markets" = "National",
  "economic_transformation_cfm_products_primary_market_regional_markets" = "Regional",
  "economic_transformation_cfm_products_primary_market_eu_markets"       = "EU"
)

cfmg |>
  filter(interview_type == "cfmg_executive") |>
  select(all_of(names(market_labels))) |>
  summarise(across(everything(), ~ sum(. == 1, na.rm = TRUE))) |>
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "variable", values_to = "n") |>
  mutate(market = factor(market_labels[variable],
                         levels = c("Local", "National", "Regional", "EU"))) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = n, y = market)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#2A9D8F", width = 0.55) +
  geom_text(aes(label = n), hjust = -0.3, size = 5, colour = "grey30") +
  scale_x_continuous(
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15)),
    name   = "Number of CFMGs"
  ) +
  scale_y_discrete(limits = rev) +
  labs(y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 18) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    axis.line.x.bottom = element_line(colour = "grey30", linewidth = 0.5),
    axis.text          = element_text(colour = "grey20"),
    plot.margin        = margin(10, 25, 10, 10)
  )
```

### Opportunity costs
#### Precluded activities
FGD groups identified economic activities that are precluded under CFM rules (@fig-et_precluded).

```{r}
#| label: fig-et_precluded
#| code-fold: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| fig-cap: "Economic activities precluded by CFM, as reported by FGD groups."
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 5

prefix_p <- "economic_transformation_economic_activities_precluded_cfm_"

precluded_labels <- c(
  "logging"                              = "Logging",
  "timber_harvesting"                    = "Timber harvesting",
  "hunting"                              = "Hunting",
  "charcoal_production"                  = "Charcoal",
  "commercial_mining"                    = "Mining",
  "farming"                              = "Farming",
  "livestock_grazing"                    = "Livestock grazing",
  "land_conversion"                      = "Land conversion",
  "introduction_of_invasive_species"     = "Invasive species",
  "ntfp_harvesting_of_forest_products"   = "NTFP harvesting",
  "fishing"                              = "Fishing",
  "beekeping_tradtional"                 = "Beekeeping",
  "fire_based_livelihood_activities"     = "Fire livelihoods"
)

full_p_vars <- paste0(prefix_p, names(precluded_labels))

p_freq <- cfmg |>
  filter(interview_type %in% c("cfmg_female", "cfmg_male")) |>
  select(all_of(full_p_vars)) |>
  summarise(across(everything(), ~ sum(. == 1, na.rm = TRUE))) |>
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "variable", values_to = "freq") |>
  mutate(word = precluded_labels[sub(prefix_p, "", variable, fixed = TRUE)]) |>
  filter(freq > 0) |>
  select(word, freq)

set.seed(42)
ggplot(p_freq, aes(label = word, size = freq, colour = freq)) +
  geom_text_wordcloud(eccentricity = 0.65, seed = 42) +
  scale_size(range = c(3, 22)) +
  scale_colour_gradient(low = "#F4A582", high = "#8B0000") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(plot.margin = margin(0, 0, 0, 0))
```

#### Other opportunity costs
FGD groups were asked whether CFM entails opportunity costs for the community (@fig-et_opp_costs).

```{r}
#| label: fig-et_opp_costs
#| code-fold: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| fig-cap: "Whether CFM entails opportunity costs for the community, by FGD group."
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 5

cfmg |>
  filter(interview_type %in% c("cfmg_executive", "cfmg_female",
```
