```{r}
                                "cfmg_male", "cfmg_youth")) |>
  transmute(
    group = case_match(
      interview_type,
      "cfmg_executive" ~ "Executive",
      "cfmg_female"    ~ "Female",
      "cfmg_male"      ~ "Male",
      "cfmg_youth"     ~ "Youth"
    ),
    response = case_when(
      economic_transformation_opportunity_costs_of_cfm == "yes"          ~ "Yes",
      economic_transformation_opportunity_costs_of_cfm == "no"           ~ "No",
      str_starts(economic_transformation_opportunity_costs_of_cfm, "No_") ~ "No",
      economic_transformation_opportunity_costs_of_cfm %in% c(
        "Community forest situated very far from community",
        "The Major livelihood for this community is fishing. They don't depend so much on the forest. They can still get materials for construction in the open areas"
      ) ~ "No",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  ) |>
  drop_na(response) |>
  mutate(
    group    = factor(group, levels = c("Youth", "Male", "Female", "Executive")),
    response = factor(response, levels = c("Yes", "No"))
  ) |>
  count(group, response) |>
  mutate(n_signed = if_else(response == "No", -n, n)) |>
  ggplot(aes(y = group, x = n_signed, fill = response)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6, colour = "white", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, colour = "white", linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label  = n,
        hjust  = if_else(response == "No", -0.4, -0.4),
        colour = response),
    size = 4, fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("No" = "white", "Yes" = "grey25"),
                      guide = "none") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("No" = "#E07B54", "Yes" = "#2A9D8F"),
                    guide  = guide_legend(reverse = TRUE)) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = ~abs(.x),
                     expand = expansion(add = 3),
                     name   = "Number of FGDs") +
  scale_y_discrete(expand = expansion(add = 0.5)) +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
  labs(y = NULL, fill = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 18) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    axis.line.x.bottom = element_line(colour = "grey30", linewidth = 0.5),
    axis.text          = element_text(colour = "grey20"),
    legend.position    = "bottom",
    plot.margin        = margin(10, 25, 10, 10)
  )
```

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
### Opportunity costs – FGDs – Model methods
To examine differences among FGD types in whether they reported that CFM entails opportunity costs for the community, we employed a generalised linear mixed model (GLMM) with a binomial response and random intercepts for province and CFMG nested within province. We used FGD type as the factor. We examined the significance of pairwise differences among FGD types using Tukey HSD.
:::

```{r}
#| label: model_et_fgd_opp_costs
#| code-fold: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false

test.data.opp <- cfmg |>
  filter(interview_type %in% c("cfmg_executive", "cfmg_female",
                                "cfmg_male", "cfmg_youth")) |>
  transmute(
    province, cfmg_name,
    interview_type = factor(
      case_match(
        interview_type,
        "cfmg_executive" ~ "Executive",
        "cfmg_female"    ~ "Female",
        "cfmg_male"      ~ "Male",
        "cfmg_youth"     ~ "Youth"
      ),
      levels = c("Executive", "Female", "Male", "Youth")
    ),
    empl = case_when(
      economic_transformation_opportunity_costs_of_cfm == "yes"           ~ 1L,
      economic_transformation_opportunity_costs_of_cfm == "no"            ~ 0L,
      str_starts(economic_transformation_opportunity_costs_of_cfm, "No_") ~ 0L,
      economic_transformation_opportunity_costs_of_cfm %in% c(
        "Community forest situated very far from community",
        "The Major livelihood for this community is fishing. They don't depend so much on the forest. They can still get materials for construction in the open areas"
      ) ~ 0L,
      TRUE ~ NA_integer_
    )
  ) |>
  drop_na(empl) |>
  mutate(province_cfmg = interaction(province, cfmg_name))

M9 <- glmer(empl ~ interview_type + (1 | province) + (1 | province_cfmg),
            data = test.data.opp, family = binomial)

emm.M9      <- emmeans(M9, specs = ~ interview_type, type = "response")
pairwise.M9 <- pairs(emm.M9, adjust = "Tukey")
```

```{r}
#| label: M9_et_fgd_opp_costs
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| output-fold: true
#| output-summary: "Opportunity costs – FGD model results (click to expand)"

summary(M9)
```

```{r}
#| label: pairwise_et_fgd_opp_costs
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| output-fold: true
#| output-summary: "Opportunity costs – FGD pairwise comparisons (click to expand)"

summary(pairwise.M9)
```

Individual interviewees were also asked whether CFM entails opportunity costs. @tbl-et_individual_opp_costs shows the percentage reporting this, broken down by interviewee role.

```{r}
#| label: tbl-et_individual_opp_costs
#| code-fold: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| tbl-cap: "Percentage of individual interviewees reporting that CFM entails opportunity costs for the community, by interviewee role."

cfmg |>
  filter(interview_type == "individual_interview") |>
  select(
    interviewee_role,
    empl = economic_transformation_opportunity_costs_of_cfm
  ) |>
  mutate(
    interviewee_role = case_match(
      as.character(interviewee_role),
      "cfmg_member"      ~ "Community member",
      "cfmg_exec_member" ~ "CFMG executive member",
      "trad_leader"      ~ "Traditional leader",
      "dfo"              ~ "District Forestry Officer",
      "NGO_rep"          ~ "NGO representative"
    ),
    interviewee_role = factor(interviewee_role, levels = c(
      "Community member", "CFMG executive member", "Traditional leader",
      "District Forestry Officer", "NGO representative"
    )),
    empl = case_when(
      empl == "yes"           ~ 1,
      empl == "no"            ~ 0,
      str_starts(empl, "No_") ~ 0,
      TRUE                    ~ NA_real_
    )
  ) |>
  group_by(interviewee_role) |>
  summarise(
    n             = n(),
    Opp_costs     = mean(empl, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups       = "drop"
  ) |>
  arrange(interviewee_role) |>
  gt() |>
  cols_label(
    interviewee_role = "Role",
    n                = "n",
    Opp_costs        = "Report opportunity costs"
  ) |>
  fmt_percent(
    columns  = Opp_costs,
    decimals = 0
  ) |>
  tab_header(
    title    = "Opportunity costs of CFM, as reported by individual interviewees",
    subtitle = "Values represent the % of positive responses. n = the total number of interview responses. A positive response indicates that the respondent considers CFM to entail opportunity costs for the community."
  ) |>
  opt_align_table_header(align = "left") |>
  cols_align(align = "left",   columns = interviewee_role) |>
  cols_align(align = "center", columns = c(n, Opp_costs)) |>
  tab_style(
    style     = cell_text(align = "center"),
    locations = cells_column_labels(columns = interviewee_role)
  ) |>
  sub_missing()
```

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
### Opportunity costs – Individual interviewees – Model methods
To examine differences among individual interviewee roles in whether they reported that CFM entails opportunity costs for the community, we employed a generalised linear mixed model (GLMM) with a binomial response and random intercepts for province and CFMG nested within province. We included interviewee role, interviewee gender and interviewee age as factors. To examine pairwise differences among interviewee roles, we employed Tukey HSD.
:::

```{r}
#| label: model_et_individual_opp_costs
#| code-fold: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false

test.data.opp.ind <- cfmg |>
  filter(interview_type == "individual_interview") |>
  mutate(
    empl = case_when(
      economic_transformation_opportunity_costs_of_cfm == "yes"           ~ 1,
      economic_transformation_opportunity_costs_of_cfm == "no"            ~ 0,
      str_starts(economic_transformation_opportunity_costs_of_cfm, "No_") ~ 0,
      TRUE                                                                 ~ NA_real_
    ),
    interviewee_gender = demo_information_interviewee_gender,
    interviewee_age    = demo_information_interviewee_age
  ) |>
  drop_na(empl) |>
  mutate(province_cfmg = interaction(province, cfmg_name))

M10 <- glmer(empl ~ interviewee_role + interviewee_gender + interviewee_age +
               (1 | province) + (1 | province_cfmg),
             data = test.data.opp.ind, family = binomial)

emm.M10      <- emmeans(M10, specs = ~ interviewee_role, type = "response")
pairwise.M10 <- pairs(emm.M10, adjust = "Tukey")
```

```{r}
#| label: M10_et_individual_opp_costs
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| output-fold: true
#| output-summary: "Opportunity costs – Individual model results (click to expand)"

summary(M10)
```

```{r}
#| label: pairwise_et_individual_opp_costs
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| output-fold: true
#| output-summary: "Opportunity costs – Individual pairwise comparisons (click to expand)"

summary(pairwise.M10)
```

The types of opportunity costs experienced are shown in @fig-et_opp_cost_types.

```{r}
#| label: fig-et_opp_cost_types
#| code-fold: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| fig-cap: "Types of opportunity costs experienced under CFM."
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 5

prefix_oc <- "economic_transformation_main_opportunity_costs_experienced_"

oc_labels <- c(
  "reduced_access_to_forest_resources_e_g_firewood_building_materials"    = "Reduced access",
  "time_spent_on_cfm_activities_instead_of_income_generating_work"        = "Time cost",
  "conflict_with_other_land_resource_uses"                                = "Land use conflict",
  "restrictions_on_land_use_for_farming_or_other_activities"              = "Land restrictions",
  "economic_costs_from_diverting_community_resources_to_cfm"              = "Diverting resources"
)

full_oc_vars <- paste0(prefix_oc, names(oc_labels))

binary_oc <- cfmg |>
  select(all_of(full_oc_vars)) |>
  summarise(across(everything(), ~ sum(. == 1, na.rm = TRUE))) |>
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "variable", values_to = "freq") |>
  mutate(word = oc_labels[sub(prefix_oc, "", variable, fixed = TRUE)]) |>
  filter(freq > 0) |>
  select(word, freq)

comment_oc <- cfmg |>
  select(text = economic_transformation_main_opportunity_costs_experienced_comment) |>
  filter(!is.na(text), trimws(text) != "") |>
  unnest_tokens(word, text) |>
  anti_join(stop_words, by = "word") |>
  mutate(word = case_match(
    word,
    c("charcoal", "production", "prohibition", "stopped",
      "restriction", "restrictions") ~ "Charcoal",
    "chitemene"                        ~ "Chitemene",
    "grazing"                          ~ "Grazing",
    "timber"                           ~ "Timber",
    "logging"                          ~ "Logging",
    "fruits"                           ~ "Fruits",
    "medicine"                         ~ "Medicinal plants",
    .default                           = word
  )) |>
  filter(!word %in% c("reduced", "involved", "traditional",
                       "interference", "access", "source",
                       "villages", "moment")) |>
  count(word, name = "freq")

wc_oc <- bind_rows(binary_oc, comment_oc) |>
  group_by(word) |>
  summarise(freq = sum(freq), .groups = "drop")

set.seed(42)
ggplot(wc_oc, aes(label = word, size = freq, colour = freq)) +
  geom_text_wordcloud(eccentricity = 0.65, seed = 42) +
  scale_size(range = c(3, 18)) +
  scale_colour_gradient(low = "#F4A582", high = "#8B0000") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(plot.margin = margin(0, 0, 0, 0))
```
